user_query:
We need a board-ready technical debt audit based on our current stack and recent production issues. Read all four inputs under input/:
- input/architecture.md (system overview and stack)
- input/team.json (team capacity and cost model)
- input/pain_points.txt (known issues we’ve been carrying)
- input/incidents.jsonl (recent incidents with IDs and impact)

Deliverables (write all files under output/):
1) output/report.md — a structured technical debt audit report with:
   - Executive Summary showing total debt items, estimated monthly carrying cost, and the debt-to-velocity ratio
   - Categorized and prioritized debt items by urgency buckets: Critical (Fix This Sprint), High Priority (Next 30 Days), Scheduled (Next Quarter), Strategic (Plan & Budget)
   - Use the 6-category taxonomy (architecture, code quality, dependencies, testing, infrastructure, documentation) and the weighted priority formula: (Risk × 3) + (Business Impact × 2) + (1/Effort × 1)
   - A phased remediation roadmap (quick wins → next 30 days → next quarter → strategic initiatives)
   - Tie each prioritized item to any relevant incident(s) by ID from input/incidents.jsonl and summarize how the debt contributed to impact
   - Clearly include the string "Debt-to-velocity ratio:" with a percentage value
2) output/backlog.csv — the prioritized backlog of debt items with the following exact columns: id, title, category, risk, business_impact, effort, priority_score, carrying_cost_hours, carrying_cost_dollars, urgency. Ensure at least one item exists for each of the 6 categories and at least 8 items total. Compute priority_score using the formula above and include carrying cost estimates derived from input/team.json. Mark quick wins (low effort, high risk) appropriately in urgency.
3) output/summary.json — a concise machine-readable summary with keys: total_items, estimated_carrying_cost_monthly_dollars, debt_to_velocity_ratio_percent, quick_wins_count. total_items must equal the number of rows in output/backlog.csv.

Assumptions are okay but state them in the report. Please ensure incident IDs from input/incidents.jsonl appear in the report where relevant.
